decisions.briefing: Markets drifted mildly lower today with NASDAQ down 0.7% and S&P 500 down 0.2% — a neutral, indecisive session with no strong directional catalyst. With only $444.94 in cash (below the $500 threshold required to open new positions), no new buys are triggered today. All six current holdings are maintained; the portfolio's +19.5% overall gain reflects solid positioning across existing names.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities edged lower Tuesday as investors weighed mixed corporate earnings results against lingering uncertainty over Federal Reserve rate policy direction. Technology stocks underperformed, pulling the NASDAQ down 0.7%, while the broader S&P 500 fell just 0.2%, suggesting selective selling in growth names rather than broad market panic. Volume was moderate and the session had a 'wait-and-see' tone as traders looked ahead to upcoming economic data.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市週二小幅收跌，投資者一方面消化好壞參半的企業財報，另一方面繼續審視聯儲局利率政策走向的不確定性。科技股表現落後，帶動納斯達克指數下跌0.7%，而標普500指數僅微跌0.2%，顯示市場以成長股的選擇性拋售為主，並非全面恐慌。整體成交量溫和，市場瀰漫觀望情緒，靜待後續重要經濟數據出爐。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a portfolio — cash earns near zero while the market moves, so idle money slowly reduces overall returns relative to a fully-invested benchmark. Holding some cash is smart for flexibility and risk management, but too much for too long causes the portfolio to lag its index. Today's $444.94 balance falls just below the $500 buy threshold, illustrating how even a small cash buffer is a deliberate trade-off between opportunity and optionality.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合持有未投資現金所帶來的機會成本——現金幾乎零回報，而市場持續波動，長期閒置資金將令整體表現落後於全額投資的基準指數。適量持有現金有助靈活應對市況及控制風險，但過多閒置現金則可能令組合跑輸指數。今日現金餘額為$444.94，略低於$500的買入觸發門檻，正好說明即使小額現金緩衝，也是在機會與靈活性之間作出的審慎取捨。
